name: ralph-tui-create-beads-rust
description: Convert PRDs to beads for ralph-tui execution using beads-rust (br CLI). Creates an epic with child beads for each user story. Use when you have a PRD and want to use ralph-tui with beads-rust as the task source. Triggers on: create beads, convert prd to beads, beads for ralph, ralph beads, br beads.
qualityReason: Clear Rust-based task automation use case
capabilities: Extracts Quality Gates from PRD documents for automated validation
Creates epic beads with external references linking back to source PRDs
Generates child beads for each user story with acceptance criteria
Appends quality gates to story acceptance criteria
Establishes dependency chains between beads (schema → backend → UI)
Exports beads for version control with br sync command
author: subsy
securitySummary: All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is documentation-only (SKILL.md) containing command examples for the legitimate beads-rust CLI tool. Scanner patterns triggered by documentation syntax, not executable code. No command injection risk as the skill provides guidance only - actual br CLI execution is controlled by the user environment.
valueStatement: Transforms product requirement documents into structured task beads for ralph-tui autonomous execution. Creates an epic with child beads for each user story, including quality gates and proper dependency ordering.
userTitle: Convert PRDs to beads for ralph-tui
limitations: Only works with beads-rust (br CLI), not the original beads (bd CLI)
Requires PRDs to follow standard markdown format with Quality Gates section
Does not execute the beads-rust CLI - only generates command syntax
Cannot verify that quality gate commands actually pass

useCases.targetUser: Product managers and team leads defining feature roadmaps
useCases.title: Agile Team Planning
useCases.description: Convert product requirement documents into actionable task breakdowns for autonomous AI agent execution. Each user story becomes a trackable bead with clear acceptance criteria and dependencies.

useCases.targetUser: Developers using ralph-tui for autonomous coding assistance
useCases.title: Automated Development Workflows
useCases.description: Set up ralph-tui to work through features incrementally. Stories execute in dependency order from schema changes through backend logic to UI components.

useCases.title: Feature Splitting
useCases.description: Transform large feature PRDs into manageable, independent stories. Ensures each story fits within a single ralph-tui iteration with no memory between runs.

promptTemplates.title: Basic PRD Conversion
promptTemplates.prompt:
Convert this PRD to beads for ralph-tui using beads-rust:

[PASTE PRD CONTENT HERE]

Extract Quality Gates, create an epic, and generate child beads for each user story. Include dependencies and quality gates in acceptance criteria.
promptTemplates.scenario: Converting a standard PRD document to structured beads

promptTemplates.title: PRD with Custom Quality Gates
promptTemplates.prompt:
Create beads from this PRD. The Quality Gates section is:

[QUALITY GATES]

[REMAINING PRD]

Extract the gates, create the epic, and make sure each story includes appropriate gates in its acceptance criteria.
promptTemplates.scenario: When PRD has custom quality requirements

promptTemplates.title: Large Feature Breakdown
promptTemplates.prompt:
This PRD describes a large feature. Split it into independent stories that:
- Each fit in one ralph-tui iteration
- Follow dependency order (schema → backend → UI)
- Have verifiable acceptance criteria

Convert to beads with proper dependency chains.
promptTemplates.scenario: Breaking down complex features into manageable stories

promptTemplates.title: Complete Project Setup
promptTemplates.prompt:
Convert this PRD to beads. After creating:
1. Verify all stories have quality gates
2. Check story ordering follows dependency rules
3. Ensure dependencies are set between related stories
4. Run br sync --flush-only to export

Then show me the generated br commands for review.

bestPractices: Extract Quality Gates first - they apply to every user story and ensure consistent quality across all work
Order stories by dependency - schema changes first, then backend, then UI - so earlier stories don't depend on later ones
Keep stories small enough to complete in one ralph-tui iteration - the agent has no memory between runs
antiPatterns: Creating stories that depend on other stories created later in the same PRD
Making stories too large to complete in a single iteration with no context continuity
Skipping quality gates or omitting them from acceptance criteria

faq.question: What is the difference between beads-rust and beads (Go version)?
faq.answer: beads-rust uses the `br` command with SQLite storage (.beads/*.db), while beads uses `bd` with JSONL storage (.beads/beads this.jsonl). Use skill for beads-rust, and the separate beads skill for the Go version.

faq.question: What if my PRD does not have a Quality Gates section?
faq.answer: Ask the user what commands should pass, or use sensible defaults like `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint`. Document the gates used for transparency.

faq.question: How do I handle UI stories differently from backend stories?
faq.answer: UI stories should include browser verification in their acceptance criteria. Backend stories only need the universal quality gates. Check the PRD for UI-specific requirements.

faq.question: Why is story ordering important for ralph-tui?
faq.answer: ralph-tui executes beads in dependency order. If a later story depends on an earlier one, the later story will be blocked until the dependency completes. Proper ordering prevents blocked work.

faq.question: How small should stories be for ralph-tui?
faq.answer: Each story must be completable in one iteration with no context from previous runs. Focus on single, verifiable changes like adding one column or one component.

faq.question: Does this skill execute the br commands?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates the br commands as output for the user or automation to review and execute. It provides the command syntax as documentation.
